coveo:metadata.title: Racial Disparities in Women s Heart Health A Prevention Focused Approach | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Social determinants of health affect cardiovascular disease prevention efforts. In this webinar, Dr Rachel Bond discusses the complexities underlying disparities in heart health with a particular focus on prevention and how we can better improve the cardiovascular outcomes in the most vulnerable patient populations.
Learning objectives: – Review the current state of cardiovascular disease and the impact of social determinants of health – Learn about gaps in risk assessment – Understand clinical tools that can assist in making treatment decisions

coveo:metadata.description: A discussion about hereditary cancer care in primary care.

coveo:metadata.title: The Association Between Vitamin D Levels and COVID-19 Positivity—and More | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
A new study finds SARS-CoV-2 positivity rates are higher among people with low vitamin D levels. Dr Kaufman will discuss his publication on the role of Vitamin D during the pandemic.
Learning objectives: – Review the role of vitamin D in the body and hormone deficiency – Understand the impact of vitamin D on the immune system – Find out who should be taking vitamin D – Learn about the Dr. Kaufman’s study: SARS-CoV-2 Positivity Rates and Vitamin D Levels

coveo:metadata.title: Is it an Environmental Allergy? The Role of Serum IgE Testing in the Primary Care and Pediatric Setting | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Webinar with the title "Is it an Environmental Allergy? The Role of Serum IgE Testing in the Primary Care and Pediatric Setting" presented by Dr Bob Geng and Sophia Thomas, moderated by Dr Anthony Horner.
Learning objectives: - Understand the link between respiratory allergy and astma - Gain insights into the role of allergy blood testing in relation to the skin prick for respiratory allergies - Utilize knowledge of allergy tests to determine triggers and inform patient management

coveo:metadata.title: The Continuum of Kidney Disease Care: From Identification to referral | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Chronic kidney disease (CKD) affects 37 million people in the US, but the vast majority don’t know they have the disease. Despite efforts to raise awareness and prevent or delay disease progression, very little progress has been made over the last 3 decades. In this webinar, Dr.David Wight will discuss CKD management up until the point of referral, and Dr. Minesh Rajpal will discuss what happens after referral.
Learning objectives: – Review the prevalence of chronic kidney disease and the importance of early detection – Understand the role of both the primary care physician and nephrologist in kidney disease management – Know which guideline-recommended tests to use to assess kidney function and damage – Learn about the National Kidney Foundation risk map and find out when referral is necessary

coveo:metadata.description:
Webinar with the title "SARS-CoV-2: Finding the New Normal— A Diagnostic Perspective". Presented by Dr Hema Kapoor, Dr Jay Wohlgemuth, and Dr Harvey Kaufman; moderated by Dr William Meyer III The presenters will discuss the impact of COVID-19 on patient health, SARS-CoV-2 science updates, virus mitigation tools and getting back to life.
Learning objectives: - SARS-CoV-2 science update - Impact of COVID-19 on patient health - Virus mitigation tools and getting back to life

Learning objectives: - Differentiate the best-use cases for PCR vs serology testing - Understand the clinical application of newly launched tick-borne tests - Determine the clinical implications of CPVID-19 on tick-borne illness, diagnosis, and prevalence

coveo:metadata.title: Drug Monitoring Webinar: Presumptive vs Definitive Drug Testing: What Are We Missing? | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: The objectives of this drug monitoring webinar include: - Learn why, now more than ever, drug testing needs to be incorporated into the standard of clinical care when prescribing controlled substances and assessing patients for medication adherence or drug misuse - Gain a deeper understanding of the differences between presumptive and definitive drug testing - Hear results from a recent Quest Diagnostics study using national data, which demonstrate some of the risks of relying solely on point-of-care presumptive testing as a drug monitoring solution

coveo:metadata.description: The objectives of this webinar include: • Review background, epidemiology, and language of rheumatic disease overlap • Present/review relevant guidelines for rheumatic disease diagnosis • Assess an innovative panel of relevant biomarkers for diagnosing rheumatic diseases • Review case study of patients with overlapping symptoms and clinical application of relevant biomarkers • Q&A

coveo:metadata.description: The objectives of this webinar include: • Examine current practice and guidelines in somatic oncology NGS testing • Analyze challenges in somatic oncology NGS testing • Evaluate the role of the genetic counselor in somatic and germline testing interpretation

coveo:metadata.title: Combating A Silent Killer: An Evidence-Based Update on the Treatment of Hypertension | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Hypertension mortality has been on the rise for the past decade. In this webinar, Dr. Joseph Saseen will review recent evidence evaluating the specific use of antihypertensive medications to empower clinicians to maximize their care of patients with hypertension.
Learning objectives: – Explain recommendations from the 2017 American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association guideline for treatment of patients with hypertension – Describe the evidence supporting bedtime dosing of antihypertensive medications – Formulate a care plan for patients with resistant hypertension based on the 2018 American Heart Association scientific statement

coveo:metadata.title: Dementia: Prevention, Diagnosis, & Management | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
As the global population ages, dementia has become a public health priority, and the World Health Organization estimates that 50 million people are living with dementia worldwide. Yet, the understanding of dementia is lacking, especially among patients. In this webinar, Dr Elizabeth Murphy Bevins will describe an evidence-based, clinical approach for the prevention and management of dementia in patients who present with cognitive complaints.
Learning objectives: – Define dementia – Propose a diagnostic algorithm – Highlight atypical clinical features warranting further investigation – Propose strategies for prevention – Describe current, evidence-based therapeutics – Discuss novel biomarkers & potential therapies on the horizon

coveo:metadata.title: Second Chances: Real Life Challenges in Secondary Prevention of ASCVD | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease is the result of oxidation, inflammation, and endothelial dysfunction caused by risk factors that are unique to each patient. In order to modify a patient’s risk for cardiovascular disease, we need to first identify and treat the underlying risk factors. In this webinar, Dr Nicole Ciffone will review guideline-supported medical management and case studies to address the challenges of controlling and reducing risk factors for high-risk and secondary prevention.
Learning objectives: – Identify patients with a history of ASCVD who are at increased risk for recurrent events – Review case studies of how guideline-based therapies could be applied – Review currently available therapies with Cardiovascular Outcome Trials (CVOT) that have demonstrated benefit to reduce recurrent events – Address important lifestyle considerations in management of patient to optimize care

The objectives of this webinar include:
• Providing an overview of post-transplant infections
• Sharing post-transplant infection trends from a national reference lab
• Reviewing details of new specialized testing services offered by Quest Diagnostics for post-transplant infection management

coveo:metadata.description:

• Enhancing clinical knowledge of respiratory allergy testing through ImmunoCAPTM IgE blood testing
• Outlining the importance and value of whole and component testing
• Demonstrating the roadmap for diagnosis and management of respiratory allergy
• Learning about the interpretation of respiratory allergy results for patient management

coveo:metadata.description:
The objectives of this webinar include:
• Hear results from the recent Quest Diagnostics Health Trends report which combines data from a Harris Poll survey of over 500 primary care physicians, with results of clinical drug laboratory testing performed by Quest Diagnostics across the US
• Gain a deeper understanding of the contributing factors to the significant surge in drug overdose deaths that we have seen in the US
• Learn about barriers health professionals face in their ability to monitor and intervene with their patients at risk for drug misuse

coveo:metadata.description:
Webinar with the title Assessing Alzheimer's Disease Risk in Primary Care Settings.
The objectives of this webinar include:
• Discuss the rising prevalence of Alzheimer’s disease
• Outline current challenges in assessing and monitoring Alzheimer’s disease
• Illustrate the role of blood-based biomarkers in primary care settings
• Explore emerging treatments and new diagnostic pathways

coveo:metadata.title: Tick-borne Illness A Panel Discussion About Emerging Diagnostics Insights | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Want to learn more about tick-borne illness? Listen to the panel discussion from Quest Diagnostics on tick-borne illness, including the serious health risks of undetected illness, why modified two-tier testing (MTTT) for Lyme disease may be more advantageous than standard two-tier testing (STTT), and which tests are appropriate in certain clinical scenarios.
The objectives of this webinar include: • Understanding the difference between MTTT and STTT for Lyme disease • Understanding the value of using Quest panels to help differentiate and account for coinfection • Differentiating when to use polymerase chain reaction (PCR) versus serology testing • Reviewing current improvements to tick-borne disease testing and new tests

coveo:metadata.description:
The objectives of this webinar include:
• Discuss the utility of food component testing for diagnosis and management
• Review lab report interpretations for food component testing: egg, milk, peanuts, and tree nuts
• Apply knowledge of component testing through case studies

coveo:metadata.title: Innovations within Precision Oncology Evolution of a Biomarker a Case Study of KRAS-G12C | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Quest Diagnostics Oncology Associate Medical Director Mark Kruzel, MD, MBA recently discussed the evolving biomarker landscape for non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) during the University of Miami Pathology Conference 2022.
Dr. Kruzel’s presentation, available on video, raises awareness of KRAS G12C as an actionable biomarker for NSCLC, and reviews available methods and clinical guidance recommendations for biomarker testing at the diagnosis of NSCLC.

- Describe the roles of biomarkers in endurance race training - Describe common biomarkers for various stages of training - Explain the advantages and limitations of using biomarkers - Have an understanding of what is currently available and whether it is right for me

coveo:metadata.title: How to Close Gaps in Care by Maximizing the Power of Laboratory Stewardship Solutions | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Experts from Quest Diagnostics and hc1 will share their lab stewardship strategies with hospitals and health systems including:
• The 4 basic elements of an effective Laboratory Stewardship program • Key criteria when building a Laboratory Stewardship committee • 3 ways lab stewardship solutions improve patient care and outcomes • How to identify low impact versus high impact interventions

coveo:metadata.title: Early Identification of Type 2 Diabetes Risk for the Primary Care Setting | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
The culmination of progressive metabolic conditions, including the obesity epidemic, has led to increasing rates of type 2 diabetes. Identifying individuals at risk of progressing to diabetes has become increasingly important. In this webinar, Dr Michael McPhaul will discuss how appropriate laboratory testing will allow for the early identification of metabolic dysfunction in order to successfully halt and prevent many chronic conditions.
Learning objectives:
– Understand that insulin resistance (IR) is a condition that precedes prediabetes – Learn how the challenges of measuring IR have been met and addressed – Discuss how metabolic conditions contribute to cardiovascular disease, chronic kidney disease, and nonalcoholic fatty liver disease – Learn the latest recommendations from the US Preventive Services Task Force on screening for prediabetes and type 2 diabetes

coveo:metadata.description:

This on-demand webinar will discuss: • The background of CLL/SLL • Diagnostic criteria • Prognostic markers • A Case study

coveo:metadata.title: Unwinding with DDX41 A case presentation regarding DDX41 and its germline predisposition to myeloid neoplasms | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Do you want to learn more about DDX41 and its germline predisposition to myeloid neoplasms?
This on-demand webinar will discuss: • DDX41: the most common gene mutation in hereditary myeloid neoplasms in adults • A newly recognized WHO category: “Myeloid Neoplasms with Germline Predisposition” • How mutations predispose to myeloid and lymphoid neoplasms • Asymptomatic carriers and the importance regarding bone marrow transplant donor selection
This is a webinar from a live webinar series developed by Quest Diagnostics and the US Oncology Network (USON)

This webinar will discuss: • An overview of myeloid neoplasms (MPN, MDS, and AML) • Diagnostic features • Molecular signatures • A case study

coveo:metadata.description: This webinar will provide an update on the current state of pharmacogenomics and the new and revised guidelines published by Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium (CPIC) to help identify ways PGx can be brought into the forefront of clinical practice.

coveo:metadata.description: In this webinar you will learn about the new era of precision medicine and what it means in terms of diagnoses and outcomes, understand why hospitals should have a precision medicine program and the economic benefits of having one, and discover how one health system is successfully utilizing precision medicine in their growing oncology program.

coveo:metadata.title: Mutated Citrullinated Vimentin Antibody as an Alternative Marker for RA | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: This webinar will provide: • Description of alternative markers to enhance diagnostic sensitivity in rheumatoid arthritis (RA). • Discussion of the variety of antibodies directed against citrullinated proteins in RA. • Review of the advantages of testing for mutated citrullinated vimentin (MCV) antibody.

coveo:metadata.title: Revolutionizing Alzheimer’s Disease Clinical Trials Using a Blood-Based Diagnostic Test for Participant Identification | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
During this webinar, esteemed Neurology expert, Dr. Michael Racke, will explore the scientific basis of the blood-based diagnostic test, its reliability and its potential impact, offering a less-invasive and more accessible tool that could transform the landscape of Alzheimer’s research.
Join this exclusive webinar and be at the forefront of the latest advancements in Alzheimer’s disease research. Discover how this blood-based diagnostic test has the power to enhance clinical trials, ushering in a new era of early intervention and hope for individuals affected by Alzheimer’s disease.

coveo:metadata.title: 2018 Webinars | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars|2018 Webinars

coveo:metadata.title: Reducing cardiovascular risk in the patient with elevated triglycerides | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
LDL cholesterol remains the principal target of therapy for patients at risk for cardiovascular disease. However, measuring atherogenic particles is a better predictor of cardiovascular disease risk than LDL cholesterol alone, particularly in patients with elevated triglycerides. In this webinar, Dr Alan Brown will review clinical implications and therapeutic approaches to patients with hypertriglyceridemia.
Learning objectives: – Understand the effect of hypertriglyceridemia on CHD risk – Review treatment strategies to reduce risk of pancreatitis and CHD events – Review appropriate therapies for patients with elevated triglycerides
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars|2017 Webinars ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars|2017 Webinars|Reducing cardiovascular risk in the patient with elevated triglycerides

coveo:metadata.title: Metabolic Basis of CVD Prevention | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Metabolic syndrome is a key risk factor for increasing atherosclerosis, yet the link between these 2 disorders often goes underrecognized. To improve treatment of patients, it is important to shed light on the metabolism of insulin resistance and its effect on inflammation and plaque formation. In this webinar, Dr Robert Eckel will discuss cardiovascular disease prevention through the assessment of metabolic risk.
Learning objectives: – Learn how metabolic factors contribute to risk of cardiovascular disease – Review lifestyle and diet considerations that reduce metabolic risk – Understand treatment strategies and goals for managing metabolic risk factors to reduce cardiovascular disease
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars|2017 Webinars ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars|2017 Webinars|Metabolic Basis of CVD Prevention

coveo:metadata.title: The Link Between Reproductive Abnormalities and Cardiometabolic Conditions | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is commonly known as a cause of infertility but what has been underappreciated is the reproductive impact on cardiometabolic health in women. In this webinar, Dr Andrea Dunaif discusses the association between common reproductive features seen in PCOS and their link to cardiometabolic risk in women throughout the lifespan.

coveo:metadata.title: Effect of Dietary Lipids on the Gut Microbiome: What Do We Know? | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: We are at the beginning stages of understanding the complexity of the gut microbiome, and novel discoveries are allowing us to capture its vast impact on many systems in the body. In this webinar, Dr Angela Zivkovic will outline what is being uncovered on the effects of dietary lipids on the gut microbiome and how, in turn, the microbiome impacts immune and metabolic function.

coveo:metadata.title: Demystifying the Diagnosis of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is a common reproductive and metabolic disorder in women that is widely underdiagnosed. In this webinar, Dr Andrea Dunaif addresses the common challenges in diagnosing PCOS and gives tips to help simplify the evaluation for providers.

coveo:metadata.title: Innovative Solutions: LC-MS/MS | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) can overcome challenges in the measurement of certain analytes to support patient care.
Learning objectives: - Explain what LC-MS/MS is and how the technology works - Discuss the benefits of using LC-MS/MS in the clinical laboratory - Identify areas when LC-MS/MS can be utilized to support patient care
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars|Innovative Solutions: LC-MS/MS

coveo:metadata.title: Early Detection Better Protection - Colorectal Cancer Screening in the Workplace: A Panel Discussion | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
A workplace colorectal screening program can fill gaps in care for those with healthcare access issues.
Colorectal screening: facts and figures: - Colorectal cancer is the third leading cause of cancer death in young adults and the top 4 leading cause of death in all cancer patient. - 1 in 5 adults with colorectal cancer are diagnosed under the age of 55. - When colorectal cancer is found and treated early, the survival rate is about 90%, that rate decreases to 13% for late-stage detection.

coveo:metadata.title: Risks and Opportunities in Risk Assessment | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: Join an evolving conversation with our newest leaders at ExamOne Keith Ward (ExamOne President) and Hope Karnes (ExamOne Medical Director). They will share some of their observations and insights about the future of laboratory testing and data solutions for the risk assessment market. They will also discuss how risks like mortality slippage and opportunities like decision support tools are impacting live insurance underwriting. How can industry counter and/or embrace these changes?

coveo:metadata.title: Quality Considerations for Mobile Blood Draws and Specimen Collections: Supporting Decentralized Studies and Trials | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
In this webinar, learn from experts with experience in leading mobile blood draws and specimen collections for a wide range of trials and studies.
What may be less common to consider — but plays a critical role in overall quality — is the protocol to ensure quality during each step of the process throughout specimen collection, processing, handling and transport that takes place prior to the specimen arriving at the lab.

coveo:metadata.title: Utilizing Apolipoprotein B in Cardiovascular Risk Assessment | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: Despite advances in understanding the pathogenesis and prevention of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease, coronary heart disease remains the leading cause of death in the United States. Apolipoprotein B (ApoB)—containing lipoproteins more closely identifies atherosclerosis and correlates to atherogenic particles in circulation.

coveo:metadata.title: Utilizing Lipoprotein(a) in Cardiovascular Risk Assessment | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: Despite advances in understanding the pathogenesis and prevention of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease, coronary heart disease remains the leading cause of death in the United States. Lipoprotein (a), or Lp(a), is a particularly atherogenic type of lipoprotein, where excess levels significantly influence cardiovascular risk.

coveo:metadata.title: Prognostic Melanoma Molecular Testing to Inform Better Patient Care | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
A clinical conversation about how prognostic molecular testing such as MelaNodal™ Predict can help assesses a patient's tumor genomics alongside clinicopathologic features to identify their risk level for sentinel lymph node (SLN) metastasis. This insight can objectively qualify which patients are at a low risk of metastasis and may avoid undergoing sentinel lymph node biopsy (SNLB).
The video features, Dr Carlos Nousari of Dermpath Diagnostics, Dr Alexander Meves of Mayo Clinic College of Medicene, and Liz Holland, RN, BSN, MFA of Quest Diagnostics.

coveo:metadata.title: Lab Testing for Overlapping Rheumatic Diseases: A Comprehensive Approach | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
This webinar will provide:
-Discussion of the evolving role of primary care providers in the diagnosis of rheumatic autoimmune diseases -Review of the advantages of testing for multiple autoimmune diseases at the same time -Understanding of the components of the ANAlyzeRTM Systemic Autoimmune Panel
This webinar was live on November 1, 2024

coveo:metadata.title: The Growing Crisis of Novel Psychoactive Substances | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Get the latest insights about the use of NPS from our nationwide analyses of drug testing samples in this on-demand webinar. Dr Jack Kain and Jason Suomala from the Quest Drug Monitoring & Toxicology team share learnings from our ongoing identification and tracking of the emerging generation of NPS, including the results of in-depth HPLC/MS analyses of thousands of drug screening samples. Quest is working to help you address the growing use of NPS and the challenges they pose for your patient drug screening and monitoring efforts.
Discussion topics include: • Overview of the various classes of new designer substances appearing across the US • Prevalence data pulled from recent laboratory testing • Review of available testing options to identify NPS use and/or exposure
Learning Objectives: • Increase your knowledge about novel Psychoactive substances (NPS), and why you should be concerned • Gain a deeper understanding of increasing NPS prevalence and characteristics of specific NPS classes and drugs • Determine clinical utility of NPS testing and share test results from an NPS case study
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars|The Growing Crisis of Novel Psychoactive Substances

coveo:metadata.title: Autoimmune Disease and Cardiometabolic Risk | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Antinuclear antibody (ANA) testing alone is not enough to definitively diagnose an autoimmune disease, because symptoms across disorders can be vague, vary from patient to patient, and overlap. Additionally, as the risk of autoimmune disease has risen among patients with history of COVID-19 infection, so has their risk for cardiometabolic disease.
This presentation will - Explore the ever-evolving role of primary care physicians in diagnosing autoimmune disease - Build confidence in results interpretation for comprehensive autoimmune testing with the ANAlyzeR™ panel - Review the necessity of monitoring and managing comorbidity risk between autoimmune disease and cardiometabolic disease
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars|Autoimmune Disease and Cardiometabolic Risk

coveo:metadata.title: Advancing Alzheimer's Diagnostics with the Power of Combined Plasma Biomarkers | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Our Quest Diagnostics Neurology team is looking forward to discussing our latest innovations and clinical offerings-providing a deeper understanding of our commitment to advancing diagnostics along the cognitive care pathway.
Multiple plasma biomarker values streamlined into a single analytical result offers a promising, scalable approach for evaluating individuals with cognitive impairment. This session will explore how combined biomarker analysis can support establishing the likelihood of amyloid pathology related to Alzheimer's disease, aiding in diagnostic decision making and care planning.

coveo:metadata.title: Early identification of cardiometabolic disease: NAFLD/MASLD & NASH/MASH | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
High rates of obesity, insulin resistance, and metabolic syndrome have led to an increase in nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD), now known as metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD), affecting roughly 80 million people in the US. Many of these patients progress to nonalcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH), now known as metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) and are at high risk of cardiovascular disease and end-stage liver disease.
This episode will • Review the prevalence and health implications of nonalcoholic fatty liver disease • Review the latest theories on the pathogenesis of fatty liver disease • Discuss guideline-recommended testing in the clinical assessment and management of nonalcoholic fatty liver disease • Review a personalized lab workup for patients with fatty liver disease
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Webinars|Early identification of cardiometabolic disease: NAFLD/MASLD & NASH/MASH

coveo:metadata.title: Comprehensive Liver Disease Risk Assessment in Primary Care: Identifying MASLD and Viral Hepatitis | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD) and viral hepatitis progress similarly in patients, beginning with clinically silent early stages, but require differential diagnosis for proper and effective treatment.
This presentation will - Review disease prevalence and progression pathways for MASLD and hepatitis - Highlight the increased risk for advanced liver fibrosis in patients with comorbid MASLD and hepatitis - Discuss screening guidelines, methodology, and testing solutions to help identify liver disease sooner

coveo:metadata.title: The Evolving Role of Biomarkers in Alzheimer’s Evaluation | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: This session is designed to leave you with better understanding of: • The evolution of the Alzheimer’s diagnostics landscape • Current performance criteria and guidelines associated with Alzheimer’s testing and staging • The practical implications of incorporating blood-based biomarker within the clinical care pathway today and in the future

coveo:metadata.title: Reducing the Tuberculosis Burden: A Focus on TB Blood Testing | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Tuberculosis (TB) cases are on the rise in the US and screening for latent and active TB is critical for decreasing the overall TB burden in the US and globally.
Many leading organizations, including CDC and USPSTF, recommend TB blood testing over skin testing, as blood or IGRA testing requires just 1 visit, 1 draw, 1 tube, saving providers and patients' time. Blood testing also performs better in patients who have received the BCG (TB) vaccine.
When determining who to screen for TB providers should think of the 3 I’s: - Immigrants - Immunocompromised patients - Individuals impacted by social and/or economic determinants of health.
Quest Diagnostics is the only national reference lab that offers both FDA approved TB blood tests: QuantiFERON TB Gold Plus, 1-tube and TSPOT TB.

coveo:metadata.title: Herpes simplex virus (HSV) diagnostics 2025: An overview | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Herpes Simplex virus type 1 (HSV-1) and type 2 (HSV-2) are two of the most common viral infections in the United States. It is estimated that 50-80% of the US population between 14 & 49 years are infected with HSV-1 and > 10% are infected with HSV-2. These infections can lead to a range of clinical manifestations and can result in lifelong complications. Laboratory diagnostics for HSV, especially for asymptomatic individuals, can be complex and challenging to interpret for both patients and clinicians.
This webinar will provide an overview of the current CDC guidelines as well as outline the testing that is available to aid in the diagnosis of genital herpes. o This presentation will - Provide an overview of HSV infections - Highlight the diagnostic considerations for genital herpes as outlined in the CDC guidelines - Review the testing that is available for HSV - Outline the diagnostic limitations and considerations with HSV testing

coveo:metadata.title: The Epidemic Within the Pandemic | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Today’s podcast will share results of a recent Quest Diagnostics Health Trends™ publication, The Opioid Epidemic Within the COVID-19 Pandemic: Drug Monitoring in 2020, which was published in the October 2020 edition of Population Health Management.
The study objective was to better understand the relationship between the COVID-19 pandemic and the drug epidemic in the US. To achieve this objective, important study results and conclusions discussed will include:
- Clinical drug testing decreased significantly during the pandemic period Nonprescribed positivity for fentanyl, heroin, and other drugs of concern increased significantly - Many dangerous drug combinations, including combinations with fentanyl, increased significantly - The impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the treatment of patients at risk for substance use disorder - The role of clinical drug monitoring
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts|The Epidemic Within the Pandemic

coveo:metadata.title: Alternative Specimen Collection Options | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: Today’s podcast discussion will focus on the importance of drug monitoring and the benefits of “alternative” sample collection options during the COVID-19 pandemic. Key information shared will include: - How clinical drug monitoring is a critical tool to help assess prescription compliance with controlled substances and help identify drug misuse - Situations, including the COVID-19 pandemic, when in-office collection isn't always possible - Quest Diagnostics’ offerings for alternative specimen collection options, including: - Self /At-Home collection - Patient Service Center collection - How alternative collection options can help ensure your patients get the testing they need

coveo:metadata.description: Today’s podcast will cover the role of clinical drug monitoring in Behavioral Health and during times of crisis. Important information shared will include: - Mental Health disorders, diagnosis and prevalence - The challenge of medication adherence in mental health treatment - The impact of crises on the treatment of patients with mental health conditions - The role of clinical drug monitoring

coveo:metadata.title: Drug Testing for Stimulants | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: Today’s podcast will cover drug testing for stimulants and will highlight some of the challenges in results interpretation for this class of drugs. Important information shared during the discussion will include: - What is classified as a stimulant - Analysis of Amphetamine, Methamphetamine, and MDMA - Interpretation challenges - Synthetic stimulants - The role of clinical drug monitoring

coveo:metadata.title: Drug Misuse in America 2019 | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Welcome to the Quest Diagnostics “topics in drug testing” podcast. Today’s episode is with Quest Diagnostics senior medical advisor, Drug Monitoring and Toxicology, Dr Jeff Gudin, and Justin Niles, lead medical informatics analyst for Quest Diagnostics Health Trends™. The podcast will cover:
Key findings from the Quest Diagnostics Health Trends report, Drug Misuse in America 2019 - Summary of analysis of more than 4.4 million de-identified drug monitoring tests performed by Quest Diagnostics from 2011 through 2018 - Findings of a Harris Poll survey of 500 primary care physicians, as part of Health - Trends report analysis - Highlights of the evolving drug misuse crisis in America, including Drug mixing and the overdose death connection - Key challenges facing physicians today, with regards to identifying drug misuse, and treating patients with chronic pain - The role and importance of drug monitoring and incorporating it into medical practice
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts|Drug Misuse in America 2019

coveo:metadata.title: Kratom: Identifying “A Drug of Concern” | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Welcome to the Quest Diagnostics “topics in drug testing” podcast. Today’s episode is with Jack Kain, PharmD, Quest Diagnostics medical science liaison, and Jeff Gudin, MD, senior medical advisor, Quest Diagnostics drug monitoring. Drs Kain and Gudin will be discussing “testing for kratom.” The podcast will cover:
- What is kratom? - What is the chemical make-up? - Kratom toxicity and side effects - Testing for kratom - Why testing is important? - How to test for kratom - What testing will tell you

coveo:metadata.title: Why and When to Use Oral Fluid Testing | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Welcome to the Quest Diagnostics “Topics in Drug Testing” podcast. Today’s episode is with Les Edinboro, PhD, Quest Diagnostics executive science director, toxicology and drug monitoring, and Jeff Gudin, MD, senior medical advisor Quest Diagnostics drug monitoring. Drs Edinboro and Gudin will be discussing “Why and When to Use Oral Fluid Testing.”
Welcome to the Quest Diagnostics “Topics in Drug Testing” podcast. Today’s episode is with Les Edinboro, PhD, Quest Diagnostics executive science director, toxicology and drug monitoring, and Jeff Gudin, MD, senior medical advisor Quest Diagnostics drug monitoring. Drs Edinboro and Gudin will be discussing “Why and When to Use Oral Fluid Testing.”
The podcast will cover: - What is oral fluid testing? - Compare oral fluid testing to other methods of testing - What are the benefits of oral fluid testing? - What are some of the drugs oral fluid testing can identify - How are the drugs identified? - How is the sample collected? - Discuss some of the collection issues
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts|Why and When to Use Oral Fluid Testing

coveo:metadata.title: Dangerous Drug Combinations | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Welcome to the Quest Diagnostics “Topics in Drug Testing” podcast. Today’s episode is with Quest Diagnostics Senior Medical Advisor Dr Jeff Gudin and Medical Science Liaison Dr Jack Kain. Drs Gudin and Kain will be discussing “Dangerous Drug Combinations.”
The podcast will cover: - What are dangerous drug combinations - What practitioners and the public need to know about them - How the drug combinations can work together to lead to side effects - Certain combinations predispose people to a greater risk of overdose and death - Can affect drug levels, creating a pharmacokinetic drug–drug interaction - May result in excessive sedation - How drug testing can help - A tool to identify dangerous drug combinations - How drug testing Identifies metabolites unique to a substance
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts|Dangerous Drug Combinations

coveo:metadata.title: Screening for Alcohol Use in Pain Management and Primary Care: It’s About Time | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Welcome to the Quest Diagnostics “Topics in Drug Testing” podcast. Today’s episode is with Quest Diagnostics Senior Medical Advisor Dr Jeff Gudin and Medical Science Liaison Dr Jack Kain. Drs Gudin and Kain will be discussing “Screening for alcohol use in pain management and primary care.”
The podcast will cover the importance of alcohol testing in order to: - Understand drug and alcohol misuse and abuse - Share prevalence rates of alcohol use and misuse, and alcohol use disorders - Provide information on alcohol use, and concomitant use with opioids and benzodiazepines - Screening guidelines - Tools and types of tests - Testing for alcohol - Breathalyzer - Advances in testing - Biomarkers - Other considerations - Quest Diagnostics approach

coveo:metadata.title: Patients with Substance Use Disorders in Medication-assisted Treatment (MAT) | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Welcome to the Quest Diagnostics “Topics in Drug Testing” podcast. Today’s episode is with Quest Diagnostics Senior Medical Advisor, Dr Jeff Gudin, and Medical Science Liaison, Dr Jack Kain. They will be discussing drug testing for patients with substance use disorders in medication-assisted treatment (MAT).
In today’s episode, some of the points that will be covered include: - What is MAT? - What are the goals of the treatment program? - The medications used in the treatment program - Drug testing during MAT - Purpose of drug testing - Types of drug testing - Specimen collection - Specimen validity - Test confirmation

coveo:metadata.title: Patient-minded Neurology | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: This episode features Dr Michael Racke discussing the evolution of treating neurologic disease to detect disease early and focus on prevention. In this session, Dr Racke discusses: - How diagnostics and treatment has evolved for multiple sclerosis - Treating patients at the first incidence of neurologic disease for optimal outcomes - How health systems can address the needs of patients with complex neurologic conditions to minimize the human and economic toll of disease
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts|Patient-minded Neurology

coveo:metadata.title: Trends in Drug Testing Resulting From the COVID-19 Pandemic | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
The convergence of the opioid epidemic and the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic has created new healthcare challenges. Health systems, from a system- and individual clinician-level, are more important than ever in the pursuit of addressing the epidemic within the pandemic.
In this session, Dr Gudin discusses: - Recently-published data by Quest that demonstrates decreased drug testing overall, with increased positivity for high-risk drugs and dangerous drug combinations - Tools and practices for clinicians to assess and stratify patients for potential prescription drug abuse - System-level strategies that employ an integrated care delivery approach, including leveraging community and diagnostics partnerships, to support impacted patient populations
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts|Trends in Drug Testing Resulting From the COVID-19 Pandemic

coveo:metadata.title: Drug Testing Your Patients for Marijuana | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Welcome to the Quest Diagnostics “topics in drug testing” podcast. Today’s episode is with Quest Diagnostics Senior Medical Advisor, Dr Jeff Gudin, and Medical Science Liaison, Dr Jack Kain. They will be discussing drug testing your patients for marijuana.
Today, we’ll be talking about marijuana and marijuana-based products in order to: - Understand drug testing for marijuana, and why it’s important - Give some general recommendations for therapeutic drug monitoring for marijuana in patients with chronic pain using controlled substances - For example, at a minimum such patients should be tested before opioid therapy begins, again within 1 to 3 months, and randomly over the next 12 months, and more frequently if there are unexpected results, or there are complaints or behavior changes in the patient

coveo:metadata.title: Optimize Oncology Care with Clinical Pathways | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: This episode features Yuri Fesko MD, with clinical pathways to help guide diagnosis and treatment in oncology. In this session, Dr Fesko discusses: - How clinical pathways can make an impact for hospitals to improve oncology outcomes - The critical importance of collaboration for pathologists and oncologists - The future of oncology care in integrated health systems
coveo:metadata.quest_category: Quest Diagnostics ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts ; Quest Diagnostics|Healthcare professionals|Clinical Education Center|Podcasts|Optimize Oncology Care with Clinical Pathways

coveo:metadata.title: COVID-19: Supporting Safety for the Front Line | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: Today’s podcast features Dr Nicholaos Bellos with strategies that are helping some health systems best operate in today's healthcare environment. In this session, Dr Bellos discusses: - The ease of viral transmission and what health systems can do to mitigate risk. - Diagnostic solutions, guidelines, and best practices to ensure safety for the front line. - The future of health systems in the post-pandemic landscape.

coveo:metadata.title: About Diagnostics Dialogues from Quest Diagnostics | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: This is the first episode in the Podcast Series "Diagnostics Dialogues from Quest Diagnostics". Dr. Pat Alagia about this podcast: "I think the podcast project gives our doctors and our scientists at Quest a voice. It connects us with the outside community. We work with a lot of folks who live in the laboratories, who live in the hospitals, who do great work here. It's essential work. Everything starts with the lab. We can't treat a patient without laboratorians and all the work that they do. We wanted to make sure that people understood how number one, important they are, but how powerful their intellects are in terms of contributing to patient care. Without them, everything stops."

coveo:metadata.title: Fentanyl: A substance of great concern | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Today’s podcast will cover a substance of great concern today, fentanyl: - Latest trends in the drug misuse epidemic in the US - What you need to know about fentanyl - Why you need to consider drug testing for fentanyl (and other illicit substances) and how to implement drug testing in your practice
This is an episode in Quest Diagnostics Podcast Series "Topics in Drug Testing"

coveo:metadata.description: Today’s podcast will cover the value of prescription and illicit drug testing and provide insight on the different clinical drug testing methodologies. We will discuss and share information regarding the following: - Current state of the drug misuse epidemic in the US - The value and clinical utility of drug testing - Overview of the 2 main methodologies of drug testing, presumptive and definitive

coveo:metadata.description: Today’s podcast discussion will focus on appropriate steps to take to implement an effective Drug Monitoring Protocol in your practice. coveo:metadata.title: The view from the top: Looking back and ahead in 2022 | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Podcast episode with the title The view from the top: Looking back and ahead in 2022, featuring Dr. Jay Wohlgemuth, senior vice president R&D at Quest Diagnostics.
In this session, Dr Alagia and Dr Wohlgemuth discuss: - The key role played by Quest in response to the COVID-19 pandemic - How Quest is applying its COVID-19 learnings to accelerate change across the healthcare industry - The unique ways in which the effectiveness of the healthcare system has and can be further improved through a consumer-centric care approach, a strategy enforced at Quest - Innovation around implementation science and strategic population health solutions available to engage consumers across all healthcare entities

coveo:metadata.title: The power of laboratory analytics | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
The power of laboratory analytics, an episode in the Diagnostics Dialogue Podcast.
In this session, Dr Waheed and Dr Hilborne discuss: - System-level strategies that can deliver meaningful, credible data to clinicians to align goals and drive meaningful, collaborative discussions across your health systems - Change management approaches that can bring together physicians, patients, laboratorians, and other key stakeholders to drive effective test utilization strategies and reduce physician burnout - Ways to approach and exercise leadership around the concept of lab stewardship within the overall delivery of care, as well as practice of pathology and laboratory medicine - Opportunities to leverage Quest’s vast patient-centric data set to inform standard of care expectations, risk stratification, and healthy equity improvements

coveo:metadata.description: Today’s podcast discussion will focus on clinical care for patients with mental health disorders. coveo:metadata.title: Contributing Factors to the Skyrocketing Drug Misuse Epidemic in the US | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Contributing Factors to the Skyrocketing Drug Misuse Epidemic in the US, an episode in Quest's Podcast Series Topics in Drug Testing.
Objectives for the podcast include: - Hear results from the recent Quest Diagnostics Health Trends report which combines data from a Harris Poll survey of over 500 primary care physicians, with results of clinical drug laboratory testing performed by Quest Diagnostics across the US - Gain a deeper understanding of the contributing factors to the significant surge in drug overdose deaths that we have seen in the US - Learn about barriers health professionals face in their ability to monitor and intervene with their patients at risk for drug misuse

coveo:metadata.description:
Episode with the title "Achieving the Triple Aim through the power of strong laboratory partnerships" in the Quest Diagnostics' Podcast Series Diagnostics Dialogues.
In this episode, Dr Pat Alagia and Mike Lukas discuss: - The clinical, operational, and business complexities health systems currently face - How strong laboratory partnerships can create sustainable healthcare delivery organizations - Important attributes of a strong partnership between laboratory diagnostics companies and health systems, and what these entities should expect from each other

coveo:metadata.title: The successful convergence of clinical and operational thinking in the lab | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Episode with the title "The successful convergence of clinical and operational thinking in the lab" in the Quest Diagnostics' Podcast Series Diagnostics Dialogues
In this episode, host Dr Pat Alagia, Senior Medical Director for Women’s and Reproductive Health at Quest Diagnostics welcomes Dr Patrick Mason and Tammy Germini to discuss: - Laboratory state-of-the-art programs and processes designed to improve quality, efficiency, and significant cost improvement outcomes - Requirements within the lab to help to achieve the successful convergence between medicine and operations, resulting in scalability of the highest performing tests - The overall value of laboratory medicine in modern healthcare

coveo:metadata.title: Alzheimer’s disease: A new generation of screenings and therapies on the horizon | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: Episode with the title Alzheimer’s disease: "A new generation of screenings and therapies on the horizon" in the Quest Diagnostics' Podcast Series Diagnostics Dialogues. In this episode, host Dr Pat Alagia, senior medical director for Women’s and Reproductive Health at Quest Diagnostics, welcomes Dr Michael K. Racke to discuss: - The current tools and methods available to physicians when diagnosing AD - The important role of blood-based biomarkers in AD and the high impact this new testing solution can have on early diagnosis, as well as management of the disease - Encouraging new treatments and diagnostic pathways for AD aiming to slow disease progression and improve quality of life - Multilayered interventions to help prevent or delay risk of AD including a healthy diet, exercise, and cognitive stimulation

coveo:metadata.description:
Podcast episode with the title "Windows of Detection" in the Topics in Drug Testing Podcast Series.
Objectives for the podcast include: - Understand drug metabolism and how prescription and illicit drugs and their metabolites are eliminated from the body - Learn about drug half-life and windows of detection, and how they are related - Gain a deeper understanding of specific variables that can impact a drug's window of detection

coveo:metadata.title: Disease Surveillance: Preventing the New Pandemic | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Podcast episode with the title "Disease Surveillance: Preventing the new Pandemic" in the Podcast Series Diagnostic Dialogues.
Today, host Dr Pat Alagia is joined by Dr Hema Kapoor and Dr Robert Jones to discuss disease surveillance: - Why did COVID take the world by surprise? - What have we learned about preventative measures and testing in order to better prepare for future pandemics like Monkeypox. - Surveillance as the key to identifying novel viruses and address privacy concerns in regards to data collection.

coveo:metadata.description:
Podcast episode with the title "Ketamine: a re-emergence in medicine and misuse?" in the Podcast Series Topics in Drug Testing, developed by Quest Diagnostics.
Learning objectives for the podcast include: - Learn how ketamine is increasingly being used as an anesthetic, analgesic, antidepressant, …and a hallucinogen1 - Gain an understanding of the risks of ketamine use and ways it is misused - Appreciate the role of drug testing when ketamine is prescribed or suspected

coveo:metadata.description: Tune in to Season 2 of Diagnostic Dialogues - Innovation & Insight and join entrepreneurial, innovative, and forward-thinking physician, Dr Pat Alagia as he speaks with top researchers, doctors, and thought leaders about the hottest topics in healthcare. From the latest in cutting-edge research to what’s coming next in the world of diagnostic medicine, this is a must hear podcast for those wanting the inside track on the engines that power the healthcare ecosystem.

coveo:metadata.title: Exploring Digital Pathology and Artificial Intelligence | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
An episode with the title "Exploring Digital Pathology and Artificial Intelligence" in the Podcast Series "Diagnostics Dialogue" (Season 2).
Today, host Dr Pat Alagia is speaking with Dr Darren Wheeler, Vice President of Pathology and Medical Services at Quest Diagnostics. Dr Wheeler joins us to discuss and examine how Quest, as the leader in advanced diagnostics, is actively participating in the creation and use of new technologies that further the mission to make precision medicine clinically accessible, actionable, and economical. Not to mention he touches on the implementation progress of digital pathology and artificial intelligence in various fields of medicine.

coveo:metadata.title: Commitment to Employee Health: A Blueprint for Wellness | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
Welcome to Diagnostics Dialogues - Innovation & Insight, where we are covering the hottest topics in healthcare with top researchers, doctors, and thought leaders! In this episode, Dr Pat Alagia is joined by Dr Steve Goldberg, Vice President, Chief Health Officer, Employee and Population Health at Quest Diagnostics.
Dr Goldberg is a brilliant physician who partners with employers, health plans, hospitals, health systems, and provider organizations to inform diagnosis and treatment to contribute to a healthier world. He sits down with Dr Alagia to discuss how diagnostics insights can help improve the health and wellbeing of both individuals and communities.

coveo:metadata.description:

This podcast episode will cover: - Summary of the 2022 CDC Clinical Practice Guideline for Prescribing Opioids for Pain - Summary of Key Findings from the Quest Diagnostics Health Trends report, Drug Misuse in America 2022—A Decade Lost to the Drug Crisis

coveo:metadata.description: In this episode of the Diagnostics Dialogues Podcast Series the importance of genetic carrier and prenatal screening, as well as Quest Diagnostic's commitment to health equity and treating women throughout every stage of their lives, is discussed. Listen to the episode where Dr Pat Alagia talks to Vice President & General Manager of Women’s and Reproductive Health at Quest Diagnostics, Kathleen Valentine, and Quest's Director and Medical Science Liaison for Women’s Health, Dr. Karen Racicot.

coveo:metadata.title: Phosphatidylethanol (PEth) blood test - Now available from Quest Diagnostics | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
This podcast episode in the topics in Drug Testing Series will cover information around PEth testing.
Jeff Gudin, MD and Jack Kain, PharmD will give an overview of the new Phosphatidylethanol (PEth) blood test from Quest Diagnostics. They will discuss Clinical Utility of PEth blood testing for Alcohol detection, and benefits of Peth blood testing vs urine testing with ethyl glucuronide (EtG) and ethyl sulfate (EtS).

coveo:metadata.title: Assessing for Alzheimer’s Disease with Quest Diagnostics | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: As the Alzheimer’s and dementia fields evolve and as new therapies come out, new diagnostic tools are also being developed with the goal of providing answers in more acceptable ways. Susan Hahn, a genetic counselor and expert in the field of Alzheimer’s disease, is joining us from Quest Diagnostics to speak on the newest risk assessment advances including some recently launched tests from Quest and what patient types they make the most sense for. Providing the patient perspective, we have Rebecca Chopp joining us from the University of Chicago. Rebecca was diagnosed with Alzheimer’s about three years ago, and currently serves on the board of Voices of Alzheimer’s as an advocate. She’ll be joining us to speak on her experience receiving a diagnosis and how she has since learned to “live with joy”.

coveo:metadata.title: Limitations of point-of-care drug testing | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description: Drug testing plays a critical role in combating the drug misuse epidemic. There are 2 main methods of clinical drug testing: presumptive testing and definitive testing. In this podcast episode, Quest Diagnostics Drug Monitoring & Toxicology experts will cover: - Overview of the different types of drug testing - Limitations of point-of-care (POC) drug testing - Quest best practices for responsible drug testing

coveo:metadata.description:
Today’s Topics in Drug Testing podcast episode will cover:
- Overview of the status of mental health in the US and mental health medication prescribing - The role of drug testing in helping to monitor medication adherence for mental health and other controlled medications as well as identify illicit substance use and potential, harmful drug-drug interactions - Implementing a responsible drug monitoring protocol in your practice, including how to test, who to test, what drugs to test for, and what frequency to test

coveo:metadata.description: Laboratory services are some of the many essential pillars supporting the preservation of maternal and fetal health. That’s why Dr. Pat Alagia and Ms. Meredith Masiello are here to share the unique services offered by Quest Diagnostics that help both clinicians and patients throughout the pregnancy journey.

coveo:metadata.description: An estimated 20 million Americans have some form of thyroid disease, and up to 60% of them are unaware of their condition. Today’s episode is with Quest Diagnostics experts Dr Sanjay Dixit, MD, medical director, and Sarah Walsh, PA-C, CLS, clinical specialist.

coveo:metadata.title: Molecular Expression Through the Eyes of the Pathologist and Oncologist | Quest Diagnostics
coveo:metadata.description:
In this installment of our two-part series on the fundamental pillars of laboratory-based diagnostic patient care, Dr. Pat Alagia is joined by two of the top minds in laboratory medicine and anatomic pathology, Dr. Hope Karnes and Dr. Darren Wheeler.
Offering a deep dive into the evolving world of pathology, they bring to light how the work of pathologists extends far beyond the microscope, spotlighting their integral roles in diagnosing patients and managing clinical laboratories.
